name: azure-mgmt-applicationinsights-dotnet
description: Manage Azure Application Insights resources for application performance monitoring and observability using the .NET SDK
capabilities: Generate code to create and configure Application Insights components
Create API keys for programmatic telemetry access
Set up web tests and availability monitoring
Build workbooks for custom dashboards and analysis
Manage linked storage accounts for data export
List and manage Application Insights resources
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is documentation-only containing Azure Application Insights SDK usage examples for .NET. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 security patterns detected. The SKILL.md file contains only code examples demonstrating legitimate Azure resource management operations. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or external command execution is present in the skill itself.
valueStatement: Azure developers need application performance monitoring but struggle with complex SDK documentation. This skill provides clear, actionable examples for managing Application Insights resources using the Azure.ResourceManager.ApplicationInsights .NET SDK.
userTitle: Manage Azure Application Insights with .NET SDK
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and appropriate permissions
Only supports Azure Resource Manager operations, not telemetry ingestion
Does not replace the Microsoft.ApplicationInsights telemetry SDK

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Setting Up Monitoring
useCases.description: A DevOps engineer needs to provision Application Insights for a new ASP.NET Core application. The skill generates code to create workspace-based components, configure retention settings, and set up API keys for integration.

useCases.title: Developer Creating Availability Tests
useCases.description: A developer wants to monitor their web application uptime. The skill provides examples for creating URL ping tests and multi-step web tests from multiple geographic locations.

useCases.title: SRE Building Custom Dashboards
useCases.description: A site reliability engineer needs custom performance dashboards. The skill shows how to create workbooks with KQL queries for requests, exceptions, and performance metrics.

promptTemplates.title: Create Application Insights Component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Azure Application Insights component in my resource group using the .NET SDK. Use workspace-based configuration with 90-day retention and production tags.

promptTemplates.title: Set Up Web Test
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate code to create a URL ping web test that checks my homepage every 5 minutes from 5 global locations. Include retry logic and alerting configuration.

promptTemplates.title: Build Performance Workbook
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a workbook with KQL queries showing requests per hour, exception rates, and response time percentiles. Use the Application Insights SDK for .NET.

promptTemplates.title: Configure API Key for Telemetry Access
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate code to create an API key with read permissions for the Application Insights API. Include error handling for 409 conflicts and proper secret management guidance.

bestPractices: Use workspace-based Application Insights components for the latest features and better integration with Log Analytics
Store connection strings and API keys in Azure Key Vault or use managed identities instead of hardcoding credentials
Configure appropriate data retention periods to balance cost requirements with debugging needs
Enable sampling for high-volume applications to reduce ingestion costs while maintaining statistical accuracy
antiPatterns: Do not commit instrumentation keys or connection strings to source control
Avoid using classic (non-workspace) Application Insights components as they are being deprecated
Do not set retention to maximum without considering cost implications for high-volume applications
Avoid creating web tests with too-frequent intervals which can cause unnecessary load and costs

faq.question: What is the difference between Application Insights and this SDK?
faq.answer: Application Insights is Azure's APM service. This SDK (Azure.ResourceManager.ApplicationInsights) manages the Azure resources themselves, not telemetry. Use Microsoft.ApplicationInsights SDK in your app to send telemetry data.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure subscription to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Creating and managing Application Insights resources requires an active Azure subscription with appropriate permissions on the target resource group.

faq.question: What authentication method does the SDK use?
faq.answer: The examples use DefaultAzureCredential which supports multiple authentication methods including managed identity, Azure CLI credentials, Visual Studio credentials, and service principals.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to query telemetry data?
faq.answer: This SDK manages resources but does not query telemetry. For querying, use the Azure Monitor Data Explorer API or the Application Insights REST API with an API key.

faq.question: What is workspace-based Application Insights?
faq.answer: Workspace-based stores data in a Log Analytics workspace. It is the current standard with better KQL query capabilities, longer retention options, and unified logging and monitoring.

faq.question: How do I delete an Application Insights component?
faq.answer: Use the DeleteAsync method on the component resource. Be aware this permanently deletes all associated telemetry data. Consider disabling or exporting data before deletion.
